footer.blurb: Codex-built tools for the people defending the open society. Lawyer-in-the-loop. Telemetry off by default.

userMenu.signOutBody: You'll be redirected to {provider} to end your session. Any case in flight stays saved on the server — you can pick it up from the Cases tab when you sign back in.

signIn.title: Welcome to Verda.
signIn.subtitle: Authenticate through your organisation's identity provider. Verda federates Keycloak, Auth0, Microsoft Entra, Google, GitHub, SAML and more — pick a provider and continue.
signIn.errorHint: Try {resetCmd} if you've changed the bundled realm, or {statusCmd} to confirm Keycloak is reachable.
signIn.demoBody: Seeded into the bundled Keycloak realm on first {makeCmd}. Pick one and click Continue with Keycloak above.
signIn.footerExtra: Adding a provider takes one entry in {file} and one in the backend Verda providers registry.
signIn.authDisabledBody: Set {frontendVar} on the frontend and {backendVar} on the backend, then bring up Keycloak ({makeCmd}), and reload.

folders.tooltip: Folders group cases by client, jurisdiction, or theme. Drag any case row onto a folder card to file it. Folders can be nested arbitrarily.
folders.emptyRoot: No folders at the root yet. Click + New folder to organise cases.
folders.emptySubfolder: This folder has no subfolders yet.

tableCaption.tooltipSuffix: Use ⌘K (or Ctrl+K) anywhere in the app to search every case in the tree, including the ones already filed inside other folders.

cases.empty: No cases here yet. Drop a folder on the upload zone above to create one, or open a folder to see what's filed inside.

table.title: Title
table.updatedTooltip: Last activity timestamp — file upload, plan change, generation run, or rename. Hover the value to see the full ISO date.

row.exportDisabledHint: Approve the plan and run generation before exporting.

export.status: Plan approved + toolkit generated. Pick a target below.
export.statusTooltip: Each target writes a standalone artifact to disk. Encrypted requires a passphrase ≥ 8 chars; Verda never stores it.
export.passphrasePlaceholder: Required for the encrypted target only
export.passphraseError: Encrypted target requires a passphrase of at least 8 characters.

createFolder.title: New folder
createFolder.body: Group cases by client, jurisdiction, or theme. Folders can be nested arbitrarily.
createFolder.label: Folder name

renameFolder.title: Rename "{name}"
renameFolder.body: Folder contents and audit trail are unaffected.
renameFolder.label: Folder name

deleteFolder.title: Delete "{name}"?
deleteFolder.body: Subfolders are deleted with it. Cases inside any of these folders are NOT deleted — they return to the root listing.

renameCase.title: Rename case
renameCase.body: The case timestamp suffix (e.g. ' · 5/5/2026') is preserved automatically.
renameCase.label: Case title

deleteCase.title: Delete case "{title}"?
deleteCase.body: Files, plan, run history, and generated artifacts are removed. Audit log entries for this case are kept (audit is append-only). This cannot be undone.

caseList.emptyBody: Drop a case folder on the upload zone to get started — Verda will classify the evidence and draft a plan.

hero.body: Drop a defender's case folder. Verda parses the evidence, drafts the petition, links Kenya Law authorities, and emits a toolkit the advocate can audit, edit, and file. Open-source. Self-hostable. Lawyer signs.

impact.body: Verda is built in partnership with {partner}. OSF underwrites the Kenya Article 22 / 23 track and the East-Africa expansion (Uganda, Tanzania) shipping next, with the roadmap reviewed quarterly alongside their strategic-litigation team and the advocates they fund.

start.title: Start a case
start.body: Drop a case folder. Verda classifies the evidence, drafts a toolkit plan, and waits for your approval before generating.
start.anonymousTitle: Sign in to view your cases.
start.anonymousBody: Verda authenticates every action. Once you sign in, your case list and the upload zone activate.
start.backendDownTitle: Could not reach the Verda backend.

procedural.name: Procedural Engine
procedural.desc: Article 22 / 23 state machine, deadlines, drafted motions.

precedent.name: Precedent Linker
precedent.desc: Ranked Kenya Law results, every URL verified.

lawyer.title: Lawyer in the loop
lawyer.bodyPrefix: Every output appears in a diff view. The lawyer reviews, edits by hand, or re-runs generation.
lawyer.bodySuffix: markers on every drafted motion enforce the boundary.

evidenceTypes.title: Ten evidence kinds, parsed without a chatbot.
evidenceTypes.body: A real Kenyan case file is mostly scanned police OBs, WhatsApp exports, and Swahili voice notes — not clean English contracts. Verda detects the kind, classifies provenance, and produces a per-case parser the lawyer can step through line by line.

about.title: Software for the people defending the open society.
about.intro1: Verda is a Codex-native platform that takes a defender's messy case file — PDFs, photos, Swahili audio, WhatsApp exports — and autonomously generates a custom litigation toolkit in code. Each toolkit contains four modules built specifically for that case: an Evidence Codex parser, a Procedural Engine state machine, a Precedent Linker, and a Defender Safety Build (offline-deployable Docker / USB). Lawyers don't get a chatbot — they get working software, generated in under an hour.
about.intro2: The platform is open-core and federated. Each partner organisation runs their own Verda instance. No central database holds case data. The core team supports orgs through documentation, templates, and packaged updates — not by operating their data.
about.intro3Prefix: Verda is built from Nairobi by a developer with shipped products in Kenyan civic spaces — Somo (CBC AI tutor) and Lipa (M-Pesa escrow) — for the partners building the Open Society. The first jurisdiction is Kenya. East Africa expansion (Uganda, Tanzania) is a content task: a local lawyer authors an
about.intro3Suffix: with template motions and procedural rules, and Verda immediately supports cases there.

intake.label: Intake
intake.body: Next.js + Tailwind upload UI. Multi-modal: PDFs, photos, audio, WhatsApp exports, plain text.

knowledge.label: Knowledge
knowledge.body: SQLite (PG-shaped) + on-disk evidence store. Whisper / Tesseract integrations are stubbed for the MVP.

orchestration.label: Orchestration
orchestration.body: FastAPI orchestrator with audit log. In-process generation; Celery / Redis is the obvious next step.

generation.label: Generation
generation.body: Codex Skills + AGENTS.md per jurisdiction. Deterministic fallback runs without any API key.

deployment.label: Deployment
deployment.body: Docker manifest, USB-bootable manifest, and AES-256-GCM encrypted bundles with a scrypt KDF.

about.lawyerBody: Verda produces drafts and tools. A licensed advocate signs and files. The Law Society of Kenya rules of professional conduct are met because the human lawyer is the responsible practitioner at every step.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.body: Immutable record of every MCP call, every Codex prompt, every export across this Verda instance.

upload.prompt: Drop a case folder or files here
upload.blurb: PDFs · photos · WhatsApp exports · audio · plain text. Drop a whole folder or pick files individually. Verda hashes, deduplicates, and classifies every item before drafting a plan.

folderConfirm.title: Upload this folder to Verda?
folderConfirm.bodyWithCount: Verda will hash, classify, and store every readable file in the dropped folder ({count} files). Files stay on the server you signed into until you choose to export. Continue to ingest, or cancel to discard.
folderConfirm.bodyNoCount: Verda will hash, classify, and store every readable file in the dropped folder. Files stay on the server you signed into until you choose to export. Continue to ingest, or cancel to discard.

folderPick.title: Pick a folder to upload?
folderPick.body:
You're about to upload an entire folder to Verda. Every readable file inside it (and its subfolders) will be hashed, classified, and stored under your case.

After you click Continue, your browser will show its own security confirmation — that one is enforced by the browser and we can't replace it. Approve it to actually open the folder picker.

toasts.nothingHint: The drop contained no files. If you dropped a folder, your browser may have blocked it — try the Browse buttons.
toasts.uploadFailedSignedOut: You're not signed in. Click Sign in, then retry.
toasts.successBodyOne: 1 file parsed and a plan was drafted.
toasts.successBodyOther: {count} files parsed and a plan was drafted.

palette.empty: No matches. Try searching by case name or jump back home.

actions.newCaseHint: drop files on the home page

notFound.title: That page is not on file.
notFound.body: The link is broken, the case was deleted, or you do not have access to it. Verda returns 404 (rather than 403) for cases you cannot read so case ids cannot be enumerated.

error.title: Verda hit an unexpected error.
error.body: The action you tried did not complete. Your case data is unaffected — Verda persists every change to the SQLite store before it renders. Retry the action; if it keeps failing, ask the operator to check the Verda backend log.

forbidden.title: Your role does not have permission for that action.
forbidden.body: Verda's IAM layer denied this request. Lawyer-in-the-loop actions (plan approval, encrypted export) are restricted to the {role} role. Per-case write access is also gated by ownership or membership. Ask the case owner for a member grant, or have an administrator assign you the right realm role in Keycloak.

caseHeader.rerunBody: This overwrites the existing bundle on disk. Any unsigned drafts and the petition draft will be regenerated from current evidence and the latest plan. Earlier exports you've already downloaded are unaffected.

profile.backendDownTitle: Could not reach the Verda backend.
profile.globalScopeTip: This role sees every case in the instance. Other roles only see cases they own or are members of.
profile.bioPlaceholder: No bio yet. Add one below — it appears next to your audit-log entries so the rest of the team has context for the actions you take.

avatar.removeBody: Your initial will be shown instead. You can upload a new one any time.

display.heading: Display
display.sourceTipPrefix: Identity (sub, email, realm roles) flows from your IdP and cannot be edited here.
display.sourceTipSuffix: and bio are local overrides — useful when your Keycloak account uses a username that isn't how teammates know you.
display.fieldBioHint: Visible to teammates next to your audit entries
display.bioInputPlaceholder: A short note about how you'd like to collaborate. Keep it under a paragraph.

permissions.canDoTipSuffix: . Ask an administrator to grant a different role in Keycloak to expand this list.
permissions.canDoEmpty: No actionable permissions yet — your role is read-only.
permissions.needsRoleTip: Permissions in this list need a role you don't currently hold (e.g. lawyer for plan approval, admin for global audit).
permissions.noneNeeded: You hold every permission Verda exposes today.

casesRead.label: Read cases
casesRead.hint: View case detail, evidence, plans, and generated artifacts.

casesCreate.label: Create cases
casesCreate.hint: Start a new case from a folder of evidence.

casesWrite.label: Edit cases
casesWrite.hint: Rename, upload more evidence, regenerate the plan.

casesDelete.label: Delete cases
casesDelete.hint: Hard-delete a case and its dependent rows. Audit trail is kept.

casesShare.label: Share cases
casesShare.hint: Add or remove case members (per-case ACL).

planApprove.label: Approve plans
planApprove.hint: Lawyer-in-the-loop gate that authorises generation to run.

generationRun.label: Run generation
generationRun.hint: Kick off the toolkit generator once a plan is approved.

exportsEncrypted.label: Export encrypted bundles
exportsEncrypted.hint: Build AES-256-GCM encrypted bundles for off-platform delivery.

auditCase.label: Read case audit
auditCase.hint: View the audit log for a case you can already see.

auditGlobal.label: Read global audit
auditGlobal.hint: View the platform-wide audit stream across every case.

usersRead.label: Read users
usersRead.hint: Look up another user's profile and case-membership state.

usersManage.label: Manage users
usersManage.hint: Reserved for admin operations on user records.

links.heading: Where to go from here
links.casesHint: Browse, organise, and search every case you can see.
links.auditHint: Append-only stream of every MCP call, plan change, and export.
links.approveHint: Plans pending your approval gate generation.
links.uploadHint: Drop a folder on the home page to start a new case.
